archetype_reasoning: Overwhelmingly trades near-certainty positions: open positions show prices of 0.985–0.999 across nearly every market, and resolved trades are dominated by entries at 0.94–0.999. Directionality of 0.9964 confirms almost exclusively one-sided positioning with no genuine two-way forecasting. The strategy is to collect small premiums on highly probable outcomes rather than make contested directional calls — classic yield harvesting behavior.
domain_blindspots: Trump_US: High win count is inflated by near-certainty entries, not genuine forecasting edge — lost on Judy Shelton Fed nomination at 0.996 and 0.954, and Trump China visit at 0.981
Iran_ME: Small sample (1 trade), open positions suggest overconfidence in near-certainty pricing on regime collapse and military strike scenarios
domain_summary: Apparent accuracy across all domains is driven by harvesting near-certain outcomes, not genuine domain expertise. The few losses (Shelton Fed nomination, Trump China visit) occurred precisely on near-certainty entries that failed, exposing the core risk of this strategy rather than revealing calibrated forecasting.
signal_weight_reasoning: Nearly all trades are entered at 0.92–0.999, meaning this trader is not price-discovering — they are confirming already-priced consensus. Copying these signals offers near-zero alpha and carries asymmetric downside risk when near-certain events fail to resolve as expected. The Shelton and China visit losses at near-certainty prices illustrate this tail risk clearly.
behavioural_flags: Extreme near-certainty entry bias: virtually all positions entered above 0.92
Repetitive same-market stacking: multiple entries in the same market (Epstein files x10, Shelton x2)
No evidence of mid-market or contested price entries in recent resolved trades
Open positions span many markets but all with identical yield-harvesting pricing logic
One anomalous loss at 0.87 (Trump Epstein files) suggests occasional misjudgment of near-certain outcomes
risk_patterns: Concentration in near-certainty NO positions on US political tail events — correlated failure risk if political volatility spikes
Stacking duplicate trades on same market increases exposure without diversification benefit
Shelton Fed nomination loss at 0.996 demonstrates that even sub-1% priced events resolve adversely, and position sizing at those prices leaves limited recovery margin
Open positions include two expired-looking markets (Trump out by April 30, resolves April 2026) suggesting portfolio may include stale unresolved entries

notable_calls.market: Will Trump release the Epstein files by December 19?
notable_calls.significance: Only contested-price entry visible in recent history — lost, confirming trader struggles outside yield-harvesting zone

notable_calls.market: Will Trump nominate Judy Shelton as next Fed chair?
notable_calls.significance: Near-certainty entry at 0.996 that resolved adversely — illustrates core risk of yield harvesting strategy

notable_calls.market: Will Trump visit China by March 31?
notable_calls.significance: Another high-confidence near-certainty NO that failed; shows geopolitical tail events can invalidate near-certain pricing
Largest open position by share count (11,321 shares) — anchors entire portfolio to political stability thesis

open_positions_summary: Portfolio is entirely structured around geopolitical and political stability: NO on Trump removal, NO on Iranian regime fall, NO on US-Iran war, NO on Kim Jong Un outreach, YES on Hezbollah ceasefire extension — all entered at 0.92–0.999. The implicit thesis is 'status quo persists across all major geopolitical flashpoints.' This is a highly correlated book that would suffer simultaneous losses if any major destabilizing shock occurred.
watch_items: Monitor Trump removal / impeachment news — largest single open position (11K shares at 0.998) is directly exposed
Track Iran escalation signals — multiple near-certainty NO positions on regime fall and US strikes could face simultaneous stress
Watch for any new contested-price entries (0.10–0.80) as a signal of genuine forecasting shift
Observe whether Judy Shelton / China visit losses trigger strategy adjustment or continued near-certainty stacking
Check resolution of stale-looking 'Trump out by April 30' position which appears past its resolve date
summary: This trader is a textbook yield harvester operating almost exclusively at near-certainty price points (0.92–0.999), generating modest returns by collecting small premiums on highly probable outcomes. The 80%+ accuracy across domains is entirely a function of entry price selection rather than genuine forecasting insight — there is no evidence of price discovery or contested-market conviction. Three notable losses on near-certainty entries (Shelton, Trump China visit, Epstein files at 0.87) confirm the strategy's tail risk. Signals from this trader should be treated as near-noise for STR-003 purposes, as copying near-certainty entries offers negligible alpha and non-trivial downside when consensus pricing proves wrong.
